markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games delayed Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, citing the need for additional polish after earlier pushes from 2025 into spring and then fall of that year. This extended window has heightened trader focus on high-profile entertainment milestones that could unfold beforehand, including major album drops from artists like Playboi Carti or Rihanna and potential advancements in generative AI such as a GPT-6 release. Take-Two Interactive’s upcoming earnings updates may clarify any further shifts, while the game’s massive cultural anticipation continues to frame these parallel developments as key swing factors in market sentiment.

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The May 15, 2026, mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial—after jurors deadlocked on the Jessica Mann charge—has driven the market’s strong consensus around no additional prison time. With a mixed 2025 verdict already delivering one conviction and an acquittal, plus Weinstein’s existing 16-year California sentence, traders see limited upside for further New York penalties, especially given repeated prosecutorial challenges and appeals. Pending sentencing on the prior conviction could add time, but historical patterns in similar #MeToo cases and the repeated inability to secure a unanimous rape conviction have shifted sentiment toward minimal or no incremental incarceration. Upcoming resolution of the Haley sentencing and any DA decisions on retrying Mann remain the key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes hold a narrow edge in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final against the Vegas Golden Knights due to their stronger regular-season record and depth across forward lines and defense. Carolina advanced with consistent playoff form after a 53-22-7 campaign, while Vegas leveraged a franchise-record postseason win streak and road resilience to reach the series. Game 2 momentum swung back to the Hurricanes following their overtime victory, tightening implied probabilities around roster health and home-ice factors in a best-of-seven matchup. Both clubs feature balanced attacks and goaltending that have limited opponents' high-danger chances throughout the playoffs.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks players have seized a commanding position in the 2026 NBA Finals by winning the first two games on the road against the San Antonio Spurs, including a dramatic 105-104 victory in Game 2 that extended their playoff win streak to 12. For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain sit nearly deadlocked at the top of the outright market, reflecting their exceptional squad depth, recent Nations League success, and favorable group draws in the expanded 48-team field. England, Portugal, Argentina, and Brazil follow closely behind, buoyed by established attacking talent and strong qualifying records, while Germany and the Netherlands occupy the next tier amid ongoing roster transitions. Late injury confirmations, including several high-profile absences across multiple squads, have introduced additional uncertainty without significantly shifting the hierarchy. The bunched pricing underscores the competitive balance among European and South American powers, with no single side enjoying a decisive edge as teams finalize preparations ahead of the June 11 kickoff.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party as general secretary and chairman of the Central Military Commission, with no verified internal challenges or succession triggers evident through mid-2026. The absence of an anointed successor and the dominance of Xi-aligned officials in top leadership bodies reduce near-term removal risks ahead of the 21st Party Congress. Recent developments, including the May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and Xi's public emphasis on launching the 15th Five-Year Plan, underscore his active role in setting policy priorities for 2026–2030. Trader consensus at 93% on "No" aligns with this stability, as historical patterns of centralized authority and limited elite contestation make abrupt leadership change before 2027 unlikely absent major unforeseen disruptions.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading trader consensus at 25.1 percent in this wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, driven by his record as California governor with executive experience and national visibility in the post-2024 reset. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 9.3 percent on progressive appeal and House legislative record, while Kamala Harris sits at 7.8 percent tied to prior national candidacy and name recognition. Other contenders such as Jon Ossoff, Josh Shapiro, and Pete Buttigieg differentiate through Senate or gubernatorial roles in competitive states and alignment with varied voter coalitions. With no formal declarations by mid-2026 and the 2026 midterms approaching, support consolidation would likely hinge on polling shifts, donor momentum, and primary-state outreach before Iowa caucuses.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading trader consensus at 25.1 percent in this wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, driven by his record as California governor with executive experience and national visibility in the post-2024 reset. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 9.3 percent on progressive appeal and House legislative record, while Kamala Harris sits at 7.8 percent tied to prior national candidacy and name recognition. Other contenders such as Jon Ossoff, Josh Shapiro, and Pete Buttigieg differentiate through Senate or gubernatorial roles in competitive states and alignment with varied voter coalitions. With no formal declarations by mid-2026 and the 2026 midterms approaching, support consolidation would likely hinge on polling shifts, donor momentum, and primary-state outreach before Iowa caucuses.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading trader consensus at 25.1 percent in this wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, driven by his record as California governor with executive experience and national visibility in the post-2024 reset. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 9.3 percent on progressive appeal and House legislative record, while Kamala Harris sits at 7.8 percent tied to prior national candidacy and name recognition. Other contenders such as Jon Ossoff, Josh Shapiro, and Pete Buttigieg differentiate through Senate or gubernatorial roles in competitive states and alignment with varied voter coalitions. With no formal declarations by mid-2026 and the 2026 midterms approaching, support consolidation would likely hinge on polling shifts, donor momentum, and primary-state outreach before Iowa caucuses.
